page: Amazon Web Services i4i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 15,000 GB NVMe SSD, and it supports a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with the stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU score both placing in the top 25%. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, contribute to strong results in PassMark encryption and extended instructions. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25%, while single-core CPU tasks, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations perform in the middle 50% average range.

From a resource density perspective, the combination of a large 15 TB local NVMe SSD and 512 GB of RAM offers high qualitative cost efficiency for IOPS-heavy and data-intensive workloads. The hardware profile is optimized for large-scale databases, transactional applications, data warehousing, and low-latency storage tasks. It is also suitable for multi-threaded software compilation and ray tracing workloads, which benefit from its strong multi-core capabilities.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 15,000 GB NVMe SSD, supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput, all ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, single-core execution and memory bandwidth fall within the average middle 50% range. This instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for storage-intensive applications due to its massive local NVMe capacity and balanced memory-to-core ratio. It is highly suited for transactional databases, data warehousing, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 15,000 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 15 TB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized server featuring sixty-four virtual cores, half a terabyte of memory, and fifteen terabytes of high-speed local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
15,000 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput I/O operations
512 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 8 GB per core ratio
Strong benchmark performance in multi-core processing and database operations
Baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for transactional databases, data warehousing, and multi-threaded workloads
